abstract: Abstract This research investigates the impact of algorithmic management on worker behaviors, focusing on workers' commitment to service quality and referral tendencies. Drawing upon the job demands‐resources model, we argue that high levels of algorithmic management could create hindrance demands that impede service quality and demotivate referral behaviors. We propose that high workload, as a challenge demand, buffers the negative effects of algorithmic management on worker outcomes. We find support for our proposed research model in an experiment with a sample of 1362 platform‐based food‐delivery riders. We also conduct a qualitative study with 21 riders, which provides a more nuanced understanding of how algorithmic management affects workers' attitudes, behaviors, and referral tendencies.

ob.ai_reason: 核心研究算法管理对平台工人行为的影响
ob.tldr: 基于工作需求‑资源模型，对1362名外卖骑手实验与21名骑手访谈发现，高强度算法管理产生阻碍需求，削弱服务质量与推荐意愿；高工作负荷作为挑战需求可缓冲其负面效应。揭示算法管理对平台工人行为的负面影响及其缓解机制。

title: Dark side of algorithmic management on platform worker behaviors: A mixed‐method study
title_zh: 算法管理对平台工作者行为的阴暗面：一项混合方法研究
